title: Oversized tool_result prevalence in real Claude Code sessions
purpose: Real-corpus evidence supporting the default-on flip of oversized tool_result elision (gateway.DefaultElideResultBytes). Measures how much raw material the lever has: how often a real coding session accumulates a tool_result whose text payload exceeds the 16 KB elision threshold.
headline: ~31% of 600 sampled real Claude Code sessions carry at least one oversized (>16 KB) tool_result; 386 oversized results totaling ~11.6 MB (~2.9M estimated tokens, bytes/4 proxy) of scrolled-past content that elision shrinks to head+tail.
method: A one-off scan of the operator's local session transcripts (~/.claude*/projects/**/*.jsonl, the 600 most-recent by mtime). For each transcript line's message.content[], every block of type tool_result is measured: a string content by its length, an array content by the summed length of its text blocks. A result counts as oversized when that text length exceeds the 16384-byte threshold. Token figures use the bytes/4 EstimateAnthropicTokens proxy.
honest_scope: This measures the PREVALENCE and SIZE of the elidable raw material (oversized tool_results), NOT the exact realized per-turn shed. The realized shed depends on each result's zone in the actual outbound /v1/messages body (after the cache head, outside the recent working-set window, no reachable cache_control) — reconstructing that exact body with Claude Code's cache_control placement is the named follow-on; this prevalence number is a sound lower-bound motivation, not the full tradeoff witness.

The synthetic dogfood (internal/agent TestElideShedMagnitudeOnLargeCodingSession) measures the realized shed on a crafted body (~56% of a 319 KB session); this artifact measures real prevalence. Together they justify the default-on flip alongside the adversarial-verification safety proof.
PRIVACY: only counts, byte lengths, and token estimates are recorded here — never a prompt, tool argument, result body, session id, or transcript path.
reproduce: Re-run the scan over ~/.claude*/projects/**/*.jsonl counting tool_result text payloads > 16384 bytes. A fak elide-witness subcommand that does this (and the exact-body tradeoff) is the named next step; this run was a manual scan.
